title: Palicourea trichophora
taxonomy: Palicourea trichophora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This genus belongs to the order Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the family Rubiaceae, Palicourea trichophora can be found in the Rubioideae subfamily. Palicourea trichophora is native to tropical regions, specifically in South America.
uses: Palicourea trichophora is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. It has been employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, Palicourea trichophora is thought to have antioxidant and antiparasitic effects, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential benefits and risks.

side_effects: Palicourea trichophora is a plant species that may have various side effects due to its toxic alkaloid content.
